name: Parameter inference tolerates variable inter-step timing (§5.5 timing-variance axis, offline, no LLM)
description: Third perturbed-UI-axis regression scenario for §5.5's remaining checklist item (docs/new/csimple-agent-prompt.md §5.5: 'Add at least one perturbed-UI scenario per generalization axis — position shift, label rename, timing variance'), closing out all three named axes. Exercises POST /api/skill/infer-params end-to-end with two demonstrations of the SAME task where a `wait` step's `ms` duration differs sharply between recordings (500ms vs 2000ms — e.g. the target app loaded slower the second time) while the click coordinates stay identical and the typed text varies. Asserts recorder/infer-params.js's designed behavior: timing fields are positional/non-semantic and are NEVER promoted to a ${param.x} placeholder even though they vary by 4x (so replay doesn't bake in one demo's arbitrary wait duration as a required parameter), while the semantically-varying typed text IS still correctly promoted to ${param.text} and the constant click coordinates stay literal numbers.
